title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer identified that `ContainerDefaults` doesn't set `ContainerPort.Protocol` to `TCP`, causing spurious updates every reconcile loop — the API server defaults the protocol to `"TCP"` on stored objects, but the desired spec leaves it as `""`, so the equality check always sees a diff. They request adding a loop inside `ContainerDefaults` to default empty `Protocol` fields to `corev1.ProtocolTCP`.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added a `for j := range containers[i].Ports` loop inside ContainerDefaults (podspec.go:54-58) that sets `Protocol = corev1.ProtocolTCP` when empty, matching the reviewer's exact suggestion. Updated the function's doc comment to list ContainerPort.Protocol. Added test coverage in TestContainerDefaults (podspec_test.go) with a container that has two ports — one with no protocol (asserts TCP default) and one with explicit UDP (asserts preservation) — directly addressing the reviewer's note that existing tests did not cover this gap.

resolution_summary: Added the missing ContainerPort.Protocol defaulting to ContainerDefaults in podspec.go, exactly as suggested by the reviewer. The loop sets Protocol to corev1.ProtocolTCP for any port that has an empty Protocol field. The function's doc comment was updated to mention ContainerPort.Protocol. A corresponding test case was added to TestContainerDefaults in podspec_test.go that exercises both the empty-protocol default (TCP) and an explicitly set UDP protocol.
